conditional_states.condition: Action mutates server state and must warn or queue when offline.
Action may take long enough to need progress or a cancel affordance.
Optimistic mutation can be rejected by a version/lock conflict and must roll back visibly.
The action can be rejected with 403 and must explain why rather than failing silently.
Optimistic UI shows a provisional result pending server confirmation.
In-flight spinner and success confirmation need a static equivalent.

rationale: An async action (save, delete, send, approve) must guarantee the user knows it started, finished, and whether it worked. Loading prevents duplicate triggers, disabled guards invalid contexts, and success/error are required so the outcome is never ambiguous. Optimistic flows additionally need a rollback path so a failed mutation does not leave a false success on screen.
evidence: WCAG 2.2 4.1.3 (Status Messages): success and failure of an action must be programmatically conveyed.
NN/g: every user-initiated action needs visible system feedback within ~1 second.
Optimistic-UI guidance (React Query mutations): reconcile and roll back on error to avoid phantom success.
